यहाँ वीडियो में कहे गए शब्दों का सटीक हिंदी अनुवाद (शब्द-प्रति-शब्द) दिया गया है:
हेलो। मेरा नाम हरतेज सिंह है। असीं (हम) अमृतसर तों (से) इत्थे (यहाँ) आए हां, तिन्न-चार (तीन-चार) फ्रेंड्स। ओशो ब्लिस... ते इत्थे असीं अटेंड कीता इन्हां दा (यहाँ हमने अटेंड किया इनका) मेडिटेशन कैम्प। जो कि बहुत ही सानूं अच्छा लग्ग्या इत्थे आण के (जो कि यहाँ आकर हमें बहुत ही अच्छा लगा)। वेदर बहुत अच्छा है। इन्हां दे जेड़े ऑर्गेनाइज़र ने (इनके जो ऑर्गेनाइज़र हैं), ओ बड़े अच्छे हां (हैं)। और सानूं इन्हां ने (हमें इन्होंने) तिन्न-चार ध्यान जेड़े ओशो दे आ, ओ दित्ते आ, करवाए आ (तीन-चार ध्यान जो ओशो के हैं, वो दिए हैं, करवाए हैं)।
ओशो डायनामिक, कुंडलिनी मेडिटेशन... ए बहुत चंगीआं लग्गीआं सानूं (ये हमें बहुत अच्छी लगीं)। ओदे नाल साडे (उसके साथ हमारे) मन नूं काफी शांति मिली आ। काफी स्लो डाउन होए आं असीं (काफी शांत/स्थिर हुए हैं हम)। सो मैं मन्नणा (चाहता) हां जेड़ा वी कोई (जो भी कोई) मेडिटेशन लई (के लिए)

People who try these techniques have nothing but deeply profound gratitude. Osho was way ahead of his time, creating these techniques 50-70 years ago- yet now somatic therapy is all the rage- and Osho too much? What an upside down world we live in 🙃😂. 

Osho at least created his works with teams of musicians, doctors, and psychiatrists… far greater checks than are used in today’s wellness alliances and industry. They were created from his deep understanding of psychology and the problems modern humans experience, and the need to throw it out. 

His meditations are based on his own methods he created, or sequenced meditation practices he’d discovered from all over the world. Practices like dynamic meditation (one of my favourites) used to be a one week retreat process he created, but he played with it until he could fit the core substance of the practice into 1 hour. 

Osho techniques are separate from Osho talks, though you have to be quite well versed in Osho meditations and teachings to see how they relate. 

People have a lot to say about Osho. He was controversial for his time- talking about sex in the time of the western hippy movement, in deeply conservative India, when even in the west you couldn’t say the word ‘cancer’ out loud in public because it was taboo… times have changed. Sensationalist journalism hasn’t updated in 50 years either 🥴.

People who seek self healing, without even having to tell anyone their problem, find Osho techniques and often find their liberation. 

And once you start doing these techniques, you find liberation. You feel things and experience things you didn’t know existed. You see yourself, your relationships and life in a new, more healed way. And you know this is needed in your life until your physical body can no longer do it. 

Of all the techniques I have tried, nothing compares. And when done consistently, day after day- the impact is huge. Which is why meditation camps can be powerful introductions or resets. @oshobliss_experiences holds meditation camps in and around Rishikesh. 

Follow for more updates. We would love to have you there.

✨ Meditations for Busy People ✨ 
Celebrating Suffering

Most of us only know two ways of relating to pain: resisting it or drowning in it.

Osho suggests another way: watching.

The next time sadness, illness, exhaustion, or emotional heaviness appears, pause. Instead of escaping, simply observe what is happening inside you.

Slowly, you may discover something surprising:
the suffering is there…
but so is a silent space beyond it.

👉🏻 Comment “BUSY” to explore more Meditations for Busy People 🌿

“When I say enjoy I mean watch, and you will enjoy. When I say enjoy, I mean don’t escape.” 
Osho, A Bird on the Wing, Talk #1

Modern life trains us to constantly move.

सत श्री अकाल जी सारया नूं (सभी को)। मेरा नाम अमृतपाल है जी। असीं (हम) अमृतसर तों (से) आए हां, चार फ्रेंड इत्थे। अपणी रोज़ दी ज़िन्दगी च (में) कम्म (काम) कर-करके, कर-करके बहुत सारीयां टेंशन-आं, शरीर दी थकावट हो जांदी आ, जिस तों (जिससे) आपा दूर होण वास्ते (होने के लिए) इत्थे ओशो ब्लिस च (में) आए सीगे जी।
इत्थों दा एनवायरनमेंट (यहाँ का माहौल) बहुत ज़्यादा वधिया (अच्छा), इत्थों दे (यहाँ के) लोग बहुत चंगे (अच्छे) ने। सानूं (हमें) हर तरीके दे नाल (के साथ) कोऑपरेट करदे ने। खाणा-पीणा, हर इक तरीके दी (की) फैसिलिटी इत्थे बहुत चंगी हैगी आ (अच्छी है)। जेड़ा वी कोई (जो भी कोई) अपणी बॉडी नूं (को) रीसायकल (रिचार्ज/तरोताज़ा) करण वास्ते (करने के लिए) आउणा चाहुंदा (आना चाहता) है, मेडिटेशन लई (के लिए) आउणा चाहुंदा वा (चाहता है)... ते (तो) प्लीज, हथ्थ बन्न के (हाथ जोड़कर) बेन्ती आ (प्रार्थना है) इत्थे ज़रूर पहुंचो, इक वारी (बार) आण के आनंद माॅणो (लो)।

Feel the Presence of Existence

मई 2026 ऋषिकेश ध्यान शिविर के लिए अपने मार्गदर्शकों (facilitators) से मिलें

क्रिस्टीन का जन्म ऑस्ट्रेलिया में हुआ था, और वे 2008 से आंतरिक खोज के मार्ग पर हैं, जब उन्होंने लंदन बौद्ध केंद्र (London Buddhist Centre) में ध्यान सीखना शुरू किया था।
पहले एक मानविकी (Humanities) शिक्षिका के रूप में प्रबंधन स्तर तक कार्य करते हुए, उन्हें यह एहसास हुआ कि दुनिया में बदलाव लाने का सबसे अच्छा तरीका स्वयं के भीतर से शुरुआत करना है - इसलिए 2021 में उन्होंने अपने करियर को सचेत और आध्यात्मिक कार्यों की ओर मोड़ लिया। वे ओशो ध्यान और अन्य ‘ब्रीदवर्क’ (श्वास-प्रक्रियाओं) में प्रशिक्षित हैं; और पिछले 5 वर्षों से उन्होंने लंदन (यूके), ऑस्ट्रेलिया, भारत, ओशो इंटरनेशनल मेडिटेशन रिसॉर्ट (पुणे), साथ ही ऑनलाइन माध्यम से ध्यान और ब्रीदवर्क का मार्गदर्शन किया है।
यूके में उन्होंने मनोरोग अस्पतालों से लेकर अपराधी प्रवृत्ति के बच्चों के स्कूलों, कॉर्पोरेट वेलनेस और सामुदायिक विश्राम सत्रों तक अपना काम साझा किया है। क्रिस्टीन भावनाओं की मुक्ति, अपने वास्तविक स्वरूप से जुड़ने और चेतना के साथ प्रयोग करने के लिए एक सुरक्षित वातावरण (safe space) प्रदान करती हैं, ताकि व्यक्ति स्वयं को और अस्तित्व को जानकर अधिक सशक्त बन सके.

सरजू का जन्म गुजरात (भारत) में हुआ था और वे 2008 से ओशो के संन्यासी हैं।
इससे पहले वे एक उद्यमी के रूप में ऑयल मिल और टायर प्लांट चला चुके हैं, और उन्होंने लंदन में कंस्ट्रक्शन मैनेजमेंट (निर्माण प्रबंधन) में भी काम किया है।
भारत में अपने व्यवसाय चलाने के दौरान, वे अक्सर माधवपुर, मेहसाणा और पुणे के ओशो आश्रमों में लंबे समय तक रुकते थे, जहाँ उन्हें अपने भीतर गहराई से उतरने का अवसर मिला। ‘जोरबा द बुद्धा’ की अपनी खोज के दौरान, उन्होंने ज़ेन अनुशासन के महान मूल्य को सीखा और वे इसी सिद्धांत को अपने काम में शामिल करते हैं, ताकि दूसरों को उनके अपने सत्य को खोजने और जानने में मदद मिल सके।
सरजू ने अनौपचारिक रूप से दोस्तों को श्वास तकनीक और अभ्यास सिखाने से शुरुआत की थी, जो 2022 में ओशो ध्यान के संचालन (facilitation) में बदल गया। उन्होंने लंदन, पुणे स्थित ओशो इंटरनेशनल मेडिटेशन रिसॉर्ट और पूरे भारत में कई गहन और व्यक्तिगत सत्र आयोजित किए हैं.

Take the opportunity to join us for a Humaniversity AUM Meditation during our once-a-year Gathering! 

You can hug, dance, feel, express, meet, share and celebrate with the friends of the Humaniversity.

The AUM meditation is a guided process in fourteen stages, lasting two hours. Together with the other participants, it leads you through an exploratory and healing experience of fourteen major aspects of human nature: anger, forgiveness, love, stamina, life energy, chaos, dance, sadness, laughter, sensuality, chanting, silence, respect and sharing. 

AUM stands for Awareness, Understanding and Meditation.
It is an excellent exercise to keep yourself alive, vibrant and open. The AUM is a Social Meditation – a great opportunity to meet old friends and create new friendships.

Registration is from 13:30 – 14:00, and in-check talks are from 14:00 – 14:30.
At 14:30 there is an introduction and explanation of the AUM before the meditation starts. This is the 1.5 hour version of our classic Humaniversity AUM Meditation.

Looking forward to see you,
Love Chandrika

“Don’t just sit there — meditate!” 🌩️

For many people, meditation feels difficult because the body still wants to move, release, breathe, express. But OSHO Active Meditations® don’t ask you to sit still. 

That’s exactly what Benedict shares in his experience with OSHO Dynamic Meditation® — a powerful OSHO Active Meditation designed for the modern mind and body. Instead of forcing silence, it begins with movement, breathing, catharsis, and expression… until stillness arrives naturally.

What touched him most was the contrast:
after moving freely and expressing everything, suddenly freezing became easy. The mind became silent. And the final stage of celebration felt authentic — not forced.

✨ Meditation can begin exactly where you are — restless, emotional, alive.

What if nourishment was something you could practice, not just imagine?

💛 Pause for 30 seconds: Close your eyes and notice what actually feels good in your body right now.
💛 Feel warmth: think of a person, a place or any moment that made you smile… remember as if it’s happening now.
💛 Give: a kind word, a hug, or a smile to someone nearby.

It is present long before we understand it intellectually, and it continues to shape our sense of aliveness, confidence, and connection throughout life. And yet, for many people, sexuality is also one of the places where shame, confusion, and pain have settled most deeply. 

Conscious Sexuality workshops exist because sexuality is rarely given the space it truly needs. Not a space of advice, technique, or performance, but a space of awareness, safety, and honest meetings and communication. 

At Humaniversity, sexuality work has been part of our therapeutic approach for decades. We have seen, again and again, that when sexuality is met with respect, awareness and friendship, it becomes a powerful gateway to healing, sovereignty, and joy. 
 
With love, 
Sambodhi

चुलेन ध्यान शिविर | अमूल्य पलहरू

२०८३ वैशाख १७ गतेबाट सुरु भएको चुलेन ध्यान शिविर (७ दिनको साधना) २०८३ वैशाख २३ गते सम्पन्न भयो।
नेपालमै पहिलो पटक यो गोप्य साधना सार्वजनिक रूपमा श्रध्देय गुरु छोग्याल रिन्पोछेद्वारा गराइयो।

चुलेन एउटा प्राचीन तिब्बती ऊर्जा अभ्यास हो। “चुलेन” शब्दको अर्थ हो — “सार ग्रहण गर्नु”।
यस अभ्यासले साधकलाई खाना भन्दा पनि प्रकृतिबाट सूक्ष्म पोषण र ऊर्जा ग्रहण गर्न सिकाउँछ।

यो केवल उपवास होइन — यो शरीर र मनलाई विश्राम, शुद्धिकरण र सन्तुलन प्रदान गर्ने शक्तिशाली अभ्यास हो।
चुलेनमार्फत आन्तरिक ऊर्जा मार्गहरू परिष्कृत हुन्छन् र प्राकृतिक जीवन ऊर्जासँग पुनः जोडिने अवसर मिल्छ।

साधकहरूका अनुसार उहाँहरूले गहिरो ध्यानको अनुभव गर्नुभयो र भविष्यमा पनि यस्तो साधना शिविर पुनः आयोजना गरिदिन गुरुज्यूसमक्ष विनम्र अनुरोध गर्नुभयो।

The Chulen Meditation Retreat (7-day practice), which began on Baisakh 17, 2083, concluded successfully on Baisakh 23, 2083.

For the first time in Nepal, this secret practice was offered publicly by H.E. Guru Chogyal Rinpoche. 

Chulen is an ancient Tibetan energy practice. The word “Chulen” means “extracting the essence.”
This practice teaches practitioners to receive subtle nourishment and energy from nature rather than depending only on physical food.

It is not simply fasting — it is a powerful practice that provides rest, purification, and balance to both body and mind.

Through Chulen, the inner energy channels become refined, allowing practitioners to reconnect with the natural life force.

According to the practitioners, they experienced deep states of meditation and sincerely requested the Guru to organize this meditation retreat again in the future.

जनाजा / मजार ये भी समाज की नौटंकी है विवाह की बारात की तरह । मरे हुए को क्या फर्क पड़ना है कितने आए या न आए ? 

हमारे श्याम जी जिन्होंने दो दशकों तक ध्यान शिविर कराए उनके जनाजे में भी नहीं के बराबर लोग थे । उनका तो हुआ जो हो गया पर वहां श्मशान में कोई और एक लाश जमीं पर पटक गया जो बिल्कुल नंगी और थी । उसे देख हमारा दिल दहल गया कि आदमी की कितनी बेकद्री हो सकती है ।

स्वयम्भूमा विशेष विश्व शान्ति पूजा जारी, ध्यान शिविरमा छोग्याल रिन्पोचेको प्रवचन 

काठमाडौँको स्वयम्भूस्थित नेपाल बुद्ध धर्म सेवा संघमा जेठ १२ गतेदेखि पवित्र सागा दावाको अवसरमा श्री मोक्ष धर्म द्वीप च्यारिटीको आयोजनामा विशेष विश्व शान्ति महापूजाको आयोजना भइरहेको छ । सोही क्रममा दुई दिने विशेष ध्यान शिविर अन्तर्गत छोग्याल रिन्पोचेले जेठ १५ गते शुक्रवार पहिलो दिनको प्रवचन दिनु भएको छ । शनिवार आज पनि उहाँले दिउँसो १ बजेदेखि ध्यान शिविरमा प्रवचन दिदै हुनुहुन्छ ।  

दैनिक जीवनको कोलाहल र तनावबाट टाढा रही मनलाई निर्मल, शान्त र स्वस्थ बनाउन चाहनेहरूका लागि यो शिविर फलदायी हुने आयोजकले जनाएको छ । यो कार्यक्रम केवल एउटा साधारण भेला मात्र नभएर मानिसभित्र लुकेर रहेको चेतनालाई जगाउने र मनका विभिन्न विकारहरूलाई निर्मूल पार्ने एउटा सुनौलो अवसर भएको बताइएको छ ।

शान्ति र ज्ञानका लागि एकजुट हौँ र यो शिक्षालाई आत्मसात् गरौँ“ भन्ने मूल नाराका साथ आयोजना भइरहेको यो आध्यात्मिक कार्यक्रमको अन्तिम दिन आइतवार टुल्कु डोल्पो रिन्पोचेको विशेष प्रवचन रहेको छ ।

A week at Osho Tapoban meditating and dancing 🧘‍♀️💃

After losing my wellness routine amid a hectic work schedule, I realized how much inner balance I was missing. Although my work often connects me with nature in the mountains, I had neglected essential self-care and inner engineering. This made management and focus increasingly difficult, and I recognized the need for a wellness break.

A week isn’t enough to maintain constant calm and focus, but it has instilled in me a firm commitment to dedicate at least an hour or two daily to meditation. I don't need to explain the well-known benefits of meditation, but I will emphasize that many lack the motivation and energy to start and sustain it.

In a world that feels rushed and chaotic, clarity becomes elusive, distractions multiply, discipline wanes, and stress seems endless. Do not wait for burnout⚠️take action now. Prioritize yourself and start your journey.

I fully endorse everything at Osho Tapoban International Commune , modern active meditation, community, nourishing food, the setting and much more. I could keep writing about my experience there but may be not everyone needs to hear about it. I am eager to share my insights with anyone interested to learn. Feel free to reach out.🙂

🔺Meditation is more crucial now than ever.

“I have called the psychology that is based on meditation the psychology of the buddhas. Modern psychology is the psychology of people who are asleep.

The psychology of the buddhas means that we accept man as a three-storied building. There are a few who remain only on the ground floor, only in their bodies; all their interests are centered in the body – this is the lowest life for someone to choose, as if you are living on the porch when the whole building is yours.

The second level of life is that of a well-understood mind – but who is going to understand the mind? You can see the difficulty of the psychologist: he studies the mind but if you ask who is studying the mind. Mind cannot study itself.

There must be something beyond – a witness, a watcher who studies the mind. The scientist is studying only from the outside.
Buddha accepts three steps: the body, the mind, the consciousness. Even the consciousness is only a step.

The psychology of the buddhas is comprehensive of the whole individuality of man – and it does not end there. By studying, by experiencing the body, the mind, the consciousness, and the beyond, Buddha is preparing you to dissolve into the universal.

Unless psychology can bring human beings to this oceanic experience it is immature, it has just started its ABC’s. And in the West it is going around in a circle – because you do not accept higher realities; where can you go? You are stuck with the mind.

Psychology is still a game, guesswork, with no foundation in reality.

The mystics in the East have never bothered too much about the mind; they have only developed methods to bypass the mind. Those methods are the techniques of meditation – they are just to bypass the mind.

Once you have bypassed the mind, once you can have a bird’s-eye view of your own mind, things start settling.

It is your energy that disturbs the mind, that gives it the power to be violent, to be sad, to be angry, to be hateful, to be jealous. Now you are no longer giving it any energy.

The moment mind disperses, your meditation has come to maturity. Now your meditation will be the medium, not the mind. The mind will be used as a mechanism by your meditative forces, but mind is put aside; it is no longer the master.

There is only one way to solve the mind and its problems, and that is to get out of it.

But modern psychology has no idea of where to go, so they go on around and around but they remain just ordinary beings. You don’t see the transforming presence, which all the mystics in all the countries have always radiated…."
Osho, Sermons In Stones, #14
